# pfsense_firewall_rule (Data Source)

Retrieves a single [firewall rule](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/firewall/index.html) by tracker ID. Firewall rules control traffic flow through the pfSense firewall.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `tracker` (String) Unique tracking ID for the firewall rule. Assigned automatically by pfSense.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).
- `destination_address` (String) Destination address. Can be 'any', a single IP address, a CIDR network (e.g. '10.0.0.0/24'), an alias name, or a special pfSense interface address (e.g. 'lanip', 'wanip'). Defaults to 'any'.
- `destination_not` (Boolean) Invert the destination address match.
- `destination_port` (String) Destination port or port range (e.g. '443' or '8000-9000'). Only applicable for TCP, UDP, or TCP/UDP protocols.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this firewall rule.
- `interface` (String) Network interface this rule applies to.
- `ipprotocol` (String) IP address family. Options: `inet`, `inet6`, `inet46`.
- `log` (Boolean) Log packets matched by this rule.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol. Options: `any`, `tcp`, `udp`, `tcp/udp`, `icmp`, `esp`, `ah`, `gre`, `ipv6`, `igmp`, `pim`, `ospf`, `carp`, `pfsync`. Defaults to `any`.
- `source_address` (String) Source address. Can be 'any', a single IP address, a CIDR network (e.g. '10.0.0.0/24'), an alias name, or a special pfSense interface address (e.g. 'lanip', 'wanip'). Defaults to 'any'.
- `source_not` (Boolean) Invert the source address match.
- `source_port` (String) Source port or port range (e.g. '80' or '8000-9000'). Only applicable for TCP, UDP, or TCP/UDP protocols.
- `type` (String) Rule action type. Options: `pass`, `block`, `reject`.
